evidence_quotes.quote: I ask that the Digital Fairness Act include specific provisions to protect digital game ownership and access.

paragraphs.text: As a Norwegian citizen, I strongly support the goals of the Stop Killing Games movement. Unfortunately, I have not been able to formally support the related European Citizens' Initiative due to Norway not being an EU member. However, I hope this consultation can take into account the concerns shared by many European consumers, including those of us in the EEA. I ask that the Digital Fairness Act include specific provisions to protect digital game ownership and access. Too many games today become completely unplayable after a publisher or developer shuts down their serverseven if the consumer paid full price. This is not just a loss of value, but also a cultural and digital preservation issue. At a minimum, I believe consumers should be guaranteed access to an offline mode, the right to host private servers, or access to a preservation build if a game is removed or disabled. The law should require clear and fair terms when a product might become unusable, and ensure long-term access to what consumers have paid for. Thank you for considering the voice of players and consumers. Sincerely, Kristian Simonsen
